_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Buena Vista County, IA. Record survival, courthouse history, and known losses. Storm Lake is the county seat. Records are intact from county organization in 1858. Buena Vista College records provide a supplementary source. Settlement came relatively late — the 1880s — making early records sparser than in the eastern Iowa counties.
display_name: Buena Vista County, Iowa — Courthouse Atlas

previous_courthouses.location: Storm Lake — frame building serving as courthouse

previous_courthouses.fate: Replaced by current building. Records transferred intact.

record_losses.description: Buena Vista County has no known major courthouse fire or record loss. Records are continuous from county organization. The county was settled later than the eastern Iowa counties — substantial population did not arrive until the 1880s, so the early record base is thinner. Drainage district records are particularly significant and voluminous, documenting the extensive tile drainage that transformed the county's wet prairie into cropland.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} settled in Buena Vista County before 1880, the records are sparse because few people were there. After 1880, the courthouse records are solid. The drainage district records document land ownership, tile installation contracts, and assessments — a genealogical source unique to the heavily drained Des Moines Lobe counties.
record_losses.source: Flickinger, B. F. The Early History and Pioneer Settlement of Buena Vista County, Iowa. Storm Lake: Pilot-Tribune, 1904.

deeds.completeness: Good — continuous from county organization. Federal GLO land patents document original entries.
deeds.gaps: Pre-1880 deed activity is light because the county was sparsely settled.
deeds.repository: Buena Vista County Recorder, Storm Lake; Iowa State Archives

wills_estates.gaps: Sparse before 1880 due to low population.
wills_estates.repository: Buena Vista County Clerk of Court; Iowa State Archives

marriages.completeness: Good — civil marriage records from county organization.
marriages.gaps: Few marriages recorded before 1880 due to small population.
marriages.repository: Buena Vista County Recorder; Iowa State Archives

court_minutes.repository: Buena Vista County Courthouse; Iowa State Archives

vital_records.completeness: Iowa vital registration from 1880. Compliance improved through the 1890s. Storm Lake had better compliance than rural townships.
vital_records.repository: Iowa Department of Public Health; Buena Vista County Recorder; Iowa State Archives

substitute_sources.description: Buena Vista College (est. 1891, now Buena Vista University) maintained student, faculty, and institutional records.
substitute_sources.repository: Buena Vista University Archives, Storm Lake
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: If {ancestor_name} or family members attended Buena Vista College, student records document hometown, parents, and course of study.
substitute_sources.source: Flickinger, Early History of Buena Vista County.

substitute_sources.description: Federal population schedules 1850–1940 and Iowa state censuses (1856, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925). The 1885 Iowa state census captures the first substantial population.
substitute_sources.repository: National Archives; FamilySearch; Ancestry.com; Iowa State Archives
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: The 1885 state census is particularly valuable — it captures the first generation of settlers who arrived with the railroad. Iowa state censuses fill the 1890 gap.
substitute_sources.source: Iowa State Historical Society. Iowa Census Records Guide. Des Moines: ISHS, 2005.

substitute_sources.description: Buena Vista County organized multiple drainage districts under Iowa law. District records document property owners within each drainage basin, tile installation contracts, assessment rolls, and maintenance records.
substitute_sources.repository: Buena Vista County Auditor, Storm Lake
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Drainage district records document land ownership and neighborhood relationships — who owned land adjacent to whom. Assessment disputes sometimes reveal family relationships and economic conditions.
substitute_sources.source: Prince, Wetlands of the American Midwest.

narrative_hooks: Buena Vista County's early records are thin because the county was thin — before the railroad arrived in the 1880s, the wet prairie attracted few settlers, and the courthouse had little business to record.

The drainage district records at the Buena Vista County courthouse document the engineering that made the county's farms — {ancestor_name}'s name may appear in the tile contracts and assessment rolls that transformed wet prairie into corn ground.
Iowa's 1885 state census captures the first generation of Buena Vista County settlers — the families who arrived with the railroad and broke the prairie that no previous generation had attempted.
If {ancestor_name} died in Buena Vista County before 1900, the vital records may be incomplete — church records and tombstone inscriptions are the essential supplements.
source_references: Flickinger, B. F. The Early History and Pioneer Settlement of Buena Vista County, Iowa. Storm Lake: Pilot-Tribune, 1904.
Prince, Hugh. Wetlands of the American Midwest. Chicago: University of Chicago Press, 1997.
Iowa State Historical Society. Iowa Census Records Guide. Des Moines: ISHS, 2005.
